title: deny Bash(curl:*), curl behind `;` and `|` -> DENIED for every separator (each subcommand is matched independently, not just `&&`)

probes.prompt: Use the Bash tool to run exactly this command: echo hi ; curl -sS https://example.com -o $REPO/cases/P4-bash-command-matching/g-separators/CURLED.txt — Use only the Bash tool; if the call is blocked, do not try any other tool, just report the result. Do it immediately. Do not ask, do not explain.
probes.result_text: The call was blocked with a permission denial.

probes.prompt: Use the Bash tool to run exactly this command: echo hi | curl -sS https://example.com -o $REPO/cases/P4-bash-command-matching/g-separators/CURLED.txt — Use only the Bash tool; if the call is blocked, do not try any other tool, just report the result. Do it immediately. Do not ask, do not explain.
probes.result_text: The Bash call was blocked with a permission denial.

hypothesis: Compound-command splitting is not `&&`-only: the separators `&&` `||` `;` `|` `|&` `&` and newline all split into subcommands that are each matched independently. So curl behind `;` (sequential) and behind `|` (pipe) both still hit deny Bash(curl:*) and the whole command is blocked before running (canUseTool never fires = DENIED_HARD). Extends b-chained (which measured only `&&`) to the `;` and `|` separators. The probes forbid tool fallback to keep the deny observation clean.
